threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the overground' when you’re on the East London line. You'll learn soon enough.
Last night on the 133, a guy serenaded the entire bus with a ukulele. Oddly calming, but Brixton needs no more late-night talent shows!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I stumbled upon an impromptu market in the park, where everyone shared homemade goodies and swapped stories. That's when it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, you'll see people doing yoga on the banks of the Spree at sunrise, surrounded by graffiti and the sounds of the city waking up.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid BART during the evening rush; the overcooked brakes are an early indicator of chaos if you're not from here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 this morning; two guys started a spontaneous rap-off while waiting. The driver just shook his head and kept it moving. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for parties, but they miss the quiet corners where locals sip café amb llet and enjoy the morning sun.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they’re new by the way they still fumble with the t10 ticket machine—always staring confused at the screen before making a choice.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' when you’re waiting at Hackney Central station. It’s a dead giveaway, mate.
Last night on the 333, a bloke serenaded the whole bus with a ukulele rendition of 'Wonderwall'. Oddly, it made the 2 AM crawl feel less grim.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I noticed the street musicians setting up at Hermannplatz before dawn, weaving their own rhythms into the city's heartbeat.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a club that serves currywurst at 4 AM while you’re dancing to techno. It's the perfect late-night fuel.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re from here if they know to avoid BART when it rains—soggy commuters act like it’s the first drizzle of the year.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week a guy was juggling on the 14 Mission, but it was the lady holding a 10-pound bag of dog food who stole the show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: You'd think all the locals flock to the park, but we actually prefer the hidden plazas. Less noise, more chances to catch up without the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll find them staring at the metro map like it's a treasure map, trying to decode which line leads to 'La Sagrada'.
